note: No sections defined — treat as a single looping animation over the listed frames.
playbackGuide: This config describes how to set up the sprite sheet exactly as configured in the Sorceress Sprite Analyzer. When an `animation` object is present, `animation.sections` is the authoritative list of named animation sections. Each section has a `role` (start | middle | end | custom), the `frames` it covers, its own `fps`, and three playback toggles: `loop` (repeat), `pingPong` (play forward then reversed, omitting shared endpoints, for a seamless back-and-forth), and `reverse` (play the frames backwards). The `playback` string restates those toggles in words. Sections may overlap. Custom sections also carry a `name` and `description` — treat these as gameplay tags (e.g. a "kick" section to play when the character kicks). The canonical phased flow is: play `start` once, then the `middle` section honoring its loop/pingPong, then `end` once; `animation.phases` lists which of those exist and `animation.start`/`middle`/`end` mirror them for older consumers. If no sections are defined use `defaultAnimation`. Frame numbers index the sheet left-to-right, top-to-bottom, 0-based.
